name: Cranberry Rugelach
recipeIngredient: 2 packages Cream cheese, softened (3 oz each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat together butter or margarine and cream cheese in a mixing bowl with an electric mixer on medium to high speed for 30 seconds.
2. Beat in the 2 tablespoons honey until combined.
3. Beat or stir in flour until just combined. Divide dough into four equal parts. Cover and chill for 1 to 2 hours, or until easy to handle.
4. For filling, stir together walnuts, cranberries, 1/4 cup honey, 1 tablespoon sugar, and 1 teaspoon cinnamon in a medium bowl. Set aside.
5. Line a cookie sheet with foil. Spray generously with nonstick spray coating. Set aside.
6. Roll one portion of dough on a lightly floured surface into a 9-inch circle.
7. Spread one-fourth of the filling over the circle, stopping 1/2 inch from the edge.
8. Lightly press filling into the dough. Cut pastry into 12 wedges.
9. Beginning at the wide end, roll up each wedge.
10. Place rolls, point side down, on the prepared cookie sheet. Repeat with remaining dough and filling.
11. Combine the 2 tablespoons sugar and 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon.
12. Brush rolls with milk and sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar mixture.
13. Bake in a 350°F oven for 15 to 18 minutes, or until golden. Transfer cookies to wire racks. Cool. Makes 48.
